Title: Fertilization-induced synergid cell death by RALF12-triggered ROS production and ethylene signaling

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes sensitized synergid degeneration/cell death after fertilization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significant late induction suggests increased ethylene biosynthesis in persistent synergid cells
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; correlation with ethylene treatment phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmACO5 is significantly induced at 24 HAP. 2. Its activation supports a rise in ethylene biosynthesis during late persistent synergid degeneration.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Persistent synergid cells, increased at 24 HAP
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper presents ACOs, not ACSs, as the strongly induced ethylene biosynthetic step during late degeneration

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes sensitized synergid degeneration/cell death after fertilization
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Significant late induction suggests increased ethylene biosynthesis in persistent synergid cells
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; correlation with ethylene treatment phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmACO20 is significantly induced at 24 HAP. 2. Its activation supports a rise in ethylene biosynthesis during late persistent synergid degeneration.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Persistent synergid cells, increased at 24 HAP
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ACO activation, rather than ACS induction, is highlighted in the paper as the major late ethylene biosynthesis change

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog; NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes oxidative burst linked to synergid degeneration
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate ROS-producing machinery component activated after fertilization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; pharmacological inhibition of NADPH oxidases blocks ZmRALF12-induced ROS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmRBOH3 is expressed in synergid cells and further activated at 12 HAP. 2. NADPH oxidase activity is required for ZmRALF12-triggered mitochondrial/cytosolic ROS accumulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synergid cells with increased expression after fertilization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific genetic dissection of ZmRBOH3 versus ZmRBOH4 was not performed

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog; NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes oxidative burst linked to synergid degeneration
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate ROS-producing machinery component activated after fertilization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; pharmacological inhibition of NADPH oxidases blocks ZmRALF12-induced ROS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmRBOH4 is expressed in synergid cells and further activated at 12 HAP. 2. NADPH oxidase activity is required for ZmRALF12-triggered mitochondrial/cytosolic ROS accumulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synergid cells with increased expression after fertilization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific genetic dissection of ZmRBOH4 versus ZmRBOH3 was not performed

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dramatic de novo activation during PCD execution indicates role in corpse clearance before synergid elimination
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; temporal correlation with autophagy/vacuole activation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCEP1 is de novo induced at 24 HAP. 2. Its late activation implicates it as a PCD executor in vacuole-related corpse clearance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as homolog of known Arabidopsis tapetal PCD executor CEP1

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dramatic de novo activation during PCD execution indicates role in vacuole rupture/corpse clearance before synergid elimination
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; temporal correlation with autophagy/vacuole activation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmVPEγ is de novo induced at 24 HAP. 2. Its activation supports vacuole-mediated PCD execution in the persistent synergid.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as major vacuolar protease with caspase-like activity likely contributing to SE fusion and corpse clearance

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RALF (Rapid Alkalinization Factor) secreted peptide family
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: ROS-mediated developmental programmed cell death signaling during fertilization
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: RALF–CrRLK1L–LLG signaling module upstream of mitochondrial ROS production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Persistent synergid cell, especially filiform apparatus adjacent region (FAAR)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Triggers oxidative stress, senescence, autophagy/mitophagy, and developmental programmed cell death of the persistent synergid to prevent supernumerary pollen tube attraction/polyspermy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fertilization-induced ROS production at the FAAR and promotes persistent synergid degeneration/cell death
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; immunolocalization; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; recombinant peptide application to ovules and isolated synergid cells; ROS staining; MDC autophagy staining; yeast two-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmRALF12 is the only highly induced maize RALF gene during persistent synergid degeneration. 2. Secreted ZmRALF12 accumulates at the filiform apparatus/FAAR after fertilization. 3. zmralf12 knockout abolishes fertilization-induced ROS accumulation at the FAAR. 4. Exogenous ZmRALF12 specifically triggers mitochondrial ROS, autophagy, and synergid apoptosis-like degeneration.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted/apoplastic at filiform apparatus and FAAR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: De novo induced after fertilization, strongly increased from 18 to 24 HAP in persistent synergid cells
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms upstream signal that induces target ROS accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss-of-function mutant shows dwarf phenotype with limited ovaries and kernels due to vegetative expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter in transactivation-related assays not for focal gene; endogenous context for native expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an autocrine fertilization-induced peptide signal rather than a metabolic enzyme; included here as pathway-associated core effector because it is the primary causal inducer of ROS production in results

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Synergid secretory program supporting pollen tube attraction/reception and pre-fertilization cell-wall/peptide secretion functions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates promoters of synergid-specific/predominant genes including secreted peptide genes, PMEI genes, ZmES4, ZmTIN1, ZmRTNL, ZmBCP, ZmANN4, and its own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ZmES4; CRP genes; PMEI genes; ZmTIN1; ZmRTNL; ZmBCP; ZmANN4; ZmMYB98
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains the synergid-specific gene regulatory network before fertilization; this network is shut down after fertilization before PCD activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cell- and stage-specific RNA-seq; dual-luciferase transactivation assays; heterologous tobacco epidermal transient co-expression with promoter:GFP reporters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB98 is the most highly expressed TF in maize synergid cells. 2. It positively regulates most abundantly expressed synergid-specific genes. 3. It autoregulates its own promoter. 4. Its sharp downregulation after fertilization marks collapse of the pre-fertilization synergid secretory network.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in synergid cells before pollination; continuously decreases during persistent synergid degeneration
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Synergid-specific before fertilization, strongly reduced after fertilization
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fertilization is associated with strong downregulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major regulator of the synergid secretory GRN rather than direct nutrient metabolite production
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S used for effector construct in dual-luciferase assay; endogenous promoters for target reporters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for manipulating synergid-specific expression programs and reproductive cell-type engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core regulatory gene in the study, but not directly assigned to nutrient metabolite biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Ethylene signaling and response during persistent synergid degeneration
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates downstream AP2/ERF transcription factor genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Eight AP2/ERF transcription factor genes induced at 24 HAP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ethylene signaling machinery is de novo activated after fertilization and supports sensitized synergid cell death
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; ethylene treatment phenotyping of ovules
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN3/EIL1 are de novo expressed after fertilization in persistent synergid cells. 2. Their induction coincides with activation of ethylene-responsive AP2/ERFs. 3. Ethylene treatment selectively promotes synergid degeneration, supporting functional ethylene signaling competence.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced at 24 HAP in persistent synergid cells
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller that sensitizes persistent synergid cells to death-promoting ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for manipulating fertilization recovery and pollen tube block
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats EIN3 and EIL1 together as key regulators sufficient for activation of the ethylene-response pathway

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC family transcription factors; homologs of Arabidopsis ANAC087/ANAC046
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Developmental programmed cell death and senescence program in persistent synergid cells
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes downstream PCD executor gene expression program
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PCD executors including ZmCEP1, ZmVPEγ, and nucleases were associated with the same late PCD program
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Late-induced NAC TFs are sufficient to induce senescence and ectopic cell death
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; phylogenetic analysis; Nicotiana benthamiana leaf transient expression assay for killing activity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNAC087L homologs are de novo/late induced in persistent synergid cells after 18 HAP. 2. Their timing matches PCD activation. 3. Each tested candidate is sufficient to trigger senescence and ectopic cell death in leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Delayed de novo activation after 18 HAP, strongest at 24 HAP
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Persistent synergid cells shortly before elimination
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Late fertilization-induced PCD program; oxidative stress/senescence context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key late PCD activators rather than direct metabolite regulators
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate tools to induce cell-type-specific PCD in reproductive tissues
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped because the paper presents them as a homolog set with similar expression and killing activity

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC family transcription factor; ortholog of Arabidopsis KIRA1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Senescence/developmental PCD program in persistent synergid cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: De novo activation marks onset of late synergid PCD program
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stage-specific RNA-seq; phylogenetic assignment; literature-supported orthology
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmKIL1 is activated after 18 HAP in persistent synergid cells. 2. Its induction is consistent with a senescence/PCD regulatory role in late synergid degeneration.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core late regulator identified from results; direct functional assay in this paper focused mainly on ZmNAC087L set

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Senescence/developmental PCD program in persistent synergid cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: De novo activation marks onset of late synergid PCD program
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmKIL2 is de novo activated after 18 HAP in persistent synergid cells. 2. It is part of the late NAC TF battery associated with synergid PCD.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Late activated NAC TF associated with synergid death
